::: {.hero-full}
![](images/hero.jpg)
:::

::: {.home-layout}

::: {.home-sidebar}

![](images/portrait.jpg)

::: {.profile-links-vertical}

<a href="mailto:bailey.anderson@slf.ch">
  <i class="bi bi-envelope-fill"></i>
  <span>Email</span>
</a>

<a href="https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=40Ppn9IAAAAJ" target="_blank">
  <i class="bi bi-mortarboard-fill"></i>
  <span>Google Scholar</span>
</a>

<a href="https://www.slf.ch/en/staff/anderson/" target="_blank">
  <i class="bi bi-building"></i>
  <span>WSL / ETH</span>
</a>

:::

:::

::: {.home-main}

# Bailey J. Anderson

## Hydrologist

I seek to understand why apparently similar catchments respond differently to similar forcings, and how those responses emerge from interacting hydrological processes across space and time.

[Research](research.qmd){.button} [Download CV](cv.qmd){.button}

## Research Themes

::: {.research-grid}

::: {.research-card}
[![](images/diversity.jpg){alt="Hydrological response"}](research.html#catchment-response-diversity)

::: {.research-card-text}
### [Hydrological response](research.html#catchment-response-diversity)

Why do apparently similar catchments respond differently?
:::
:::

::: {.research-card}
[![](images/extremes.jpg){alt="Variability and whiplash"}](research.html#variability-and-whiplash)

::: {.research-card-text}
### [Variability and Whiplash](research.html#variability-and-whiplash)

Understanding rapid state changes in streamflow.
:::
:::

::: {.research-card}
[![](images/processes.jpg){alt="Methodology"}](research.html#hydrological-processes)

::: {.research-card-text}
### [Methodology](research.html#hydrological-processes)

Challenging how methodological choices influence scientific conclusions.
:::
:::

:::

:::

:::
